name: Editorial Pipeline

phase_0.boundary_condition: The Frobenius closure of a provider cascade: all LLM outputs that satisfy the editorial intent, across all available providers, collapse into a single served output.
phase_0.justification: The Editorial Pipeline is the structural imscription of the auto.py LLM ob3ect-design loop applied as a general-purpose editing, parsing, and rewriting tool. The domain is bounded by the provider cascade: what the cascade can produce within the editorial intent is inside the boundary; what lies beyond any provider's capability or context is outside. The three-stage API (SET / SUBMIT / SERVE) directly mirrors the IMASM winding structure: SET binds the initial and terminal states; SUBMIT runs the Frobenius split/fuse through provider fanout; SERVE commits and delivers the fixed output.

VINIT.justification: The undifferentiated intake state — raw text with no resolved editorial transformation. No provider has seen it, no intent has been applied. Void of distinction between the current and target states.
rejected_candidates: Prompt
The prompt is upstream of the pipeline; the source text is what enters the pipeline.
Draft
Too narrow — the pipeline handles any text: documents, code comments, prose, transcripts.

TANCH.justification: The terminal boundary: the specification of what the output must be. Every edit pass moves toward it; the quality gate tests against it; the served output is anchored to it. The pipeline cannot close without an intent.
rejected_candidates: Target Document
Too specific — the intent is the constraint, not the final document itself.
Style Guide
Style guides are partial specifications; intent is the complete terminal condition.

IMSCRIB.justification: The self-imscription of the source: the pipeline reads the source text's own grammar — its outline, claims, rhetorical moves, and register — before applying any transformation. The parse is the pipeline's recognition of the source as a structured object.
rejected_candidates: Summary
A summary collapses structure; a parse preserves it.
Tokenization
Too mechanical — the parse identifies semantic and rhetorical structure, not tokens.

AFWD.justification: The forward morphism: the LLM applies the editorial intent to the parsed source structure. Moves text from VINIT toward TANCH. The first and most direct application of the cascade.
rejected_candidates: Rewrite
Rewrite is the FFUSE step — AFWD is the targeted edit, not the full reconstruction.
Summarization
Summarization destroys structure; AFWD preserves and transforms it.

AREV.justification: The reverse morphism: after the forward edit and rewrite, the pipeline checks the output against the stated intent. Identifies gaps between the served text and the terminal condition. Can trigger another CLINK → AFWD cycle.
rejected_candidates: Proofreading
Proofreading checks correctness; critique checks alignment with intent.
Feedback
Feedback is external; critique is the pipeline's own backward pass.

CLINK.justification: The sequential composition of multiple edit passes when a single pass does not fully close the gap between source and intent. CLINK chains AFWD → FFUSE → AREV → AFWD into multi-pass pipelines.
rejected_candidates: Loop
A loop implies repetition without direction; CLINK is directed composition toward the terminal state.
Batch
Batch implies parallel execution; CLINK is sequential composition.

FSPLIT.justification: The split morphism (δ): the pipeline branches across the provider cascade — local model, qwen, deepseek — running each independently. The fanout is the Frobenius split that enables multiple provider outputs to be evaluated and merged.
rejected_candidates: Parallel Query
Too generic; FSPLIT specifically models the Frobenius δ structure of the cascade.
A/B Test
A/B testing is about selection; FSPLIT is about range decomposition.

FFUSE.justification: The fuse morphism (μ): selects the best provider output and collapses the fanout back to a single canonical result. The Frobenius pair with FSPLIT: μ∘δ = id on the output space.
rejected_candidates: Ensemble
Ensemble combines all outputs; FFUSE selects and commits the best single output.
Consensus
Consensus implies averaging; FFUSE is topological closure of the cascade.

EVALT.justification: The true branch of the evaluation gate: the provider output satisfies the editorial criteria (completeness, alignment with intent, structural fidelity). The cascade closes on this branch.
rejected_candidates: Approval
Too human-centric; EVALT is a structural gate, not a preference.
Pass
Accurate but too informal for the gate's structural role.

EVALF.justification: The false branch: the provider output fails the quality criteria. The cascade advances to the next provider. If all providers fail, the pipeline sets the EVALF flag and returns the best available output.
rejected_candidates: Error
An error is a crash; EVALF is a structural branch, not an exception.
Retry
Retry implies repetition on the same provider; EVALF advances the cascade.

ENGAGR.justification: The B-state: the provider output partially satisfies the intent — some dimensions of the target state are met, others are not. The pipeline flags this as a dialetheic B-state and routes to the AREV critique pass for resolution.
rejected_candidates: Partial Match
Accurate but misses the Belnap structural identity of the B-state.
Uncertain
Uncertainty is epistemic; ENGAGR is a structural co-presence of T and F.

IFIX.justification: The irreversible fixation: the final text is locked and served. No further passes alter it. The winding number is fixed; the pipeline cycle is closed.
rejected_candidates: Final Draft
Too editorial; IFIX is the structural closure of the pipeline winding.
Output
Accurate but lacks the fixation semantics — IFIX is irreversible commitment.

phase_2.split_input: Source Text + Editorial Intent (primary edit pass output)
phase_2.fuse_result: Best-selected provider edit — single canonical output selected by quality gate
phase_2.test_instance: Source text 'The market recovered.' + intent 'make more precise' → FSPLIT branches to three providers → local: 'Markets rebounded 2.3% by close.' / qwen: 'The S&P 500 gained 1.8% on Wednesday.' / deepseek: 'Equity markets recovered losses by session end.' → EVALT selects qwen output → FFUSE collapses → FFUSE output recovers the intent 'make more precise' ✓

phase_3.void_description: No transformation — source text unchanged, intent unapplied. The cascade produced no output meeting even partial criteria. The pipeline returns with EVALF set and source unchanged.
phase_3.true_description: Successful rewrite — output fully satisfies the editorial intent with structural fidelity to the source. Quality gate accepted on first provider. IFIX committed. ΔS < 0.
phase_3.false_description: Failed rewrite — all providers in the cascade failed the quality gate. Intent cannot be achieved with the available providers for this source. Pipeline returns best available output with EVALF flag.
phase_3.both_description: Partial match B-state — output satisfies some dimensions of the editorial intent but leaves others unresolved. ENGAGR is active; AREV critique pass identifies the specific gap. May trigger CLINK → AFWD for a second pass.

transitions.trigger: provider cascade produces an accepted output
CLINK second pass resolves the gap
all providers fail on the second pass too

phase_3.entropy_assertion: ΔS < 0 — the pipeline is anti-entropic: each pass reduces structural noise, sharpens alignment with intent, and compresses the distance between source state and terminal condition.

steps.domain_action: Intake source text — initialize the pipeline winding with raw, unedited input. No provider has been invoked; no intent has been applied. The source is treated as a void-state object.
Anchor editorial intent — bind the target transformation as the terminal boundary condition. The pipeline cannot close without a stated intent; TANCH makes it the explicit topological boundary.

Parse source structure — call the primary LLM to identify: outline, main claims, rhetorical moves, sentence-level style, and register. The parse result is carried as context through all subsequent passes.

Primary edit pass — apply the editorial intent to the parsed source structure. The LLM targets the parse result directly: rewriting only what the intent specifies, preserving what is structurally sound.
Provider fanout — branch the edit prompt across the provider cascade: local model first, then qwen via OpenRouter, then deepseek. Each provider receives the same prompt independently.
Quality gate: accept — evaluate each provider output against the editorial criteria (completeness, alignment with intent, structural fidelity to source). Accept on first provider that passes.
Quality gate: reject — if the current provider output fails the criteria, advance to the next provider in the cascade. If all providers fail, set EVALF flag and proceed with best available output.
Output merge — collapse the provider fanout back to a single canonical edit. The accepted provider output becomes the input for the rewrite pass. Frobenius pair with FSPLIT closes here.

B-state flag — if the merged output partially satisfies the intent but leaves dimensions unresolved, set the ENGAGR flag. Route to AREV for a critique pass. Do not IFIX a B-state output without critique.

Critique pass — call the LLM to check the rewrite against the stated intent. Identify specific gaps: claims not addressed, register not matched, structural problems. Critique informs CLINK composition.

Pipeline composition — if critique identifies persistent gaps, compose another AFWD → FFUSE pass with the critique notes as additional context. Chain as many passes as needed until EVALT or cascade exhaustion.
Commit output — lock the final text and serve. The winding is closed; no further passes alter the committed output. The IFIX text is the pipeline's served result.

failure_modes: Cascade exhaustion: all providers fail EVALT — EVALF flag set, best available output served
Empty intent: TANCH not set before submit() — pipeline raises RuntimeError
Context overflow: source text exceeds provider context window — split source and compose outputs via CLINK

phase_5.compiler_frontend: The IMSCRIB parse step: maps raw source text to a structured grammar of claims, sections, and rhetorical moves that the AFWD edit passes can target precisely.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Provider API calls via urllib (stdlib only): QWEN_API_KEY for OpenRouter / qwen, DEEPSEEK_API_KEY for deepseek.com. Both accept OpenAI-compatible chat/completions format. Local model via llama.cpp socket if available.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: The inter-step context buffer: carries source structure (IMSCRIB parse), edit diffs (AFWD output), critique notes (AREV output), and prior pass text as additional context for each subsequent LLM call. No external store required.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Provider cascade: local model first (if socket available), then qwen via OpenRouter, then deepseek. Quality gate (EVALT/EVALF) advances the cascade on rejection. CLINK composes passes sequentially toward TANCH.
phase_5.alfs_store: Optional JSONL audit log: records source, intent, each pass output, quality gate verdicts, provider used, and IFIX committed text. Written to ~/.ob3ect/editorial_pipeline.jsonl by default.

phase_6.cycle_cost: 3–4 LLM provider calls per full pipeline run: IMSCRIB parse (1 call) + AFWD edit (1–3 calls across cascade) + FFUSE rewrite (1 call) + optional AREV critique (1 call). Token cost proportional to source length.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Raw source text with stated but unapplied editorial intent. No provider has been invoked.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: IFIX committed output: intent applied, quality-gate verified, critique-checked (if run). Provider that produced the accepted output is recorded.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS < 0 — anti-entropic lift: each pass removes noise, sharpens structure, and reduces the distance between source state and the terminal intent condition. The pipeline always moves toward TANCH.
phase_6.failure_mode: Cascade exhaustion — all three providers fail the quality gate. Pipeline sets EVALF flag and serves the least-bad output. Source is always recoverable from the serve() dict.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → IMSCRIB → AFWD → FSPLIT → EVALT → EVALF → FFUSE → ENGAGR → AREV → CLINK → IFIX
-- Class: Editorial Pipeline
-- Fingerprint: sig=(e7fa3e,)
--   auto_generated=True | period=12
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(4,7)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT      dim     := 𐑼                𐑼 → 𐑠  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] TANCH      top     := 𐑡                𐑠 → 𐑭  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [2] IMSCRIB    gram    := 𐑠                𐑭 → ⊙  | identity — self-imscription
--   [3] AFWD       rel     := 𐑾                ⊙ → 𐑾  | forward morphism — primary edit
--   [4] FSPLIT     gran    := 𐑚                𐑾 → 𐑖  | split δ — provider fanout
--   [5] EVALT      crit    := ⊙                𐑖 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — quality gate accept
--   [6] EVALF      chir    := 𐑖                𐑖 → 𐑱  | evaluate-false — quality gate reject
--   [7] FFUSE      stoi    := 𐑙                𐑙 → 𐑳  | fuse μ — output merge
--   [8] ENGAGR     stoi    := 𐑳                𐑳 → 𐑗  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [9] AREV       pol     := 𐑗                𐑗 → 𐑱  | reverse morphism — critique pass
--  [10] CLINK      fid     := 𐑱                𐑱 → 𐑡  | composition — pipeline chaining
--  [11] IFIX       prot    := 𐑭                𐑡 → 𐑼  | irreversible fixation — committed output

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def editorial_pipeline_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑠)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | source text intake
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑠 𐑭)  -- [1] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | editorial intent anchor
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑭 ⊙)  -- [2] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | structural parse
  (.arrow 𐑾 ⊙ 𐑾)  -- [3] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | primary edit pass
  -- FSPLIT [4] (gran := 𐑚) / FFUSE [7] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (EVALT accepts)
      (.arrow ⊙ 𐑖 𐑙)  -- [5] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | quality gate: accept
      -- F-branch (EVALF rejects, advances cascade)
      (.arrow 𐑖 𐑖 𐑱))  -- [6] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | quality gate: reject
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [7]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑳)  -- [7] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 | output merge
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑳 𐑗)  -- [8] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | B-state flag
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑗 𐑱)  -- [9] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | critique pass
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑱 𐑡)  -- [10] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | pipeline composition
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑡 𐑼)  -- [11] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | committed output

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def editorial_pipeline_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (editorial_pipeline_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def editorial_pipeline_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (editorial_pipeline_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem editorial_pipeline_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
notes: Auto-designed from: Editorial Pipeline — the auto.py LLM cascade imscribed as a general-purpose editing, parsing, and rewriting ob3ect. SET / SUBMIT / SERVE directly map to the IMASM winding structure.
